mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate minimizes the opportunity cost of holding equities relative to cash, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate that rises more than 0.5 percentage points above its trailing 12-month minimum signals labor market deterioration, triggering earnings recession expectations that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE exceeds trailing 12-month minimum by more than 0.5 percentage points
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and industrial production indicates demand-pull expansion outpacing supply constraints, raising input cost fears and margin compression that produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO fails to show a negative rank correlation with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes, a decline in industrial production signals stagflationary supply contraction, prompting negative equity repricing as earnings forecasts are cut while discount rates remain elevated over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in high-inflation regimes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts reflects tightening credit conditions and weakening construction demand that immediately spills into cyclical equity sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month HOUST changes are not associated with negative 5-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter print in real GDP indicates contracting aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 growth prints are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and consumption, lifting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the unemployment rate and declines in industrial production identify an early recessionary dynamic that forces negative earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of rising UNRATE and falling INDPRO does not predict negative 60-day returns with a hit rate above 55%.
